concepts_list.name: Omega_domain
concepts_list.description: The main computational domain in the physical space
concepts_list.justification: The symbol Ω is explicitly defined as the domain of interest, which is a subset of R^d, and is described as the region of interest in the problem. It is also decomposed into subdomains, indicating its role as the primary domain.

concepts_list.name: Gamma_boundary
concepts_list.description: The boundary of the domain Ω, composed of disjoint regions with different boundary conditions
concepts_list.justification: The boundary Γ is defined as the boundary of Ω with outward unit normal n, and is further decomposed into Γ_u (Dirichlet) and Γ_t (Neumann) parts, indicating it is a boundary domain.

concepts_list.name: Gamma_interface
concepts_list.description: Internal interfaces between subdomains of the domain Ω
concepts_list.justification: The text explicitly defines Γ_ij as the interface between subdomains i and j, and notes that the union of all Γ_i represents internal interfaces between subdomains.

concepts_list.name: displacement_variable
concepts_list.description: The primary unknown variable (temperature or displacement) in the problem
concepts_list.justification: The symbol u is used to denote the temperature field in the thermal problem and the displacement field in the elastostatics problem, and is referred to as the primal variable in the abstract formulation.

concepts_list.name: test_function
concepts_list.description: A test function used in the weak form of the variational problem
concepts_list.justification: The symbol v appears in all weak formulations as a test function, and is used in the inner product with the solution u, satisfying the condition for all v in V.

concepts_list.name: thermal_conductivity
concepts_list.description: The thermal conductivity tensor κ, used in the heat conduction equation
concepts_list.justification: The symbol κ is used in the weak form as the coefficient of the gradient term in the thermal problem, and is described as a given physical parameter.

concepts_list.name: heat_source
concepts_list.description: The internal heat source term f in the thermal problem
concepts_list.justification: The symbol f is explicitly described as the heat source in the thermal boundary value problem and appears in the right-hand side of the weak form equation.

concepts_list.name: heat_flux
concepts_list.description: The prescribed heat flux q_bar on the boundary Γ_t
concepts_list.justification: The symbol q_bar is introduced as the prescribed heat flux on the Neumann boundary Γ_t and appears in the boundary integral of the weak form.

concepts_list.name: function_space_V
concepts_list.description: The test function space V, where the test functions v are defined
concepts_list.justification: The symbol V is introduced as the function space for the test functions, and appears in the weak form with the condition 'for all v in V'.

concepts_list.name: function_space_U
concepts_list.description: The solution function space U, where the solution u is defined
concepts_list.justification: The symbol U is introduced as the function space for the solution, and appears in the weak form with the condition 'find u in U'.

concepts_list.name: bilinear_form_B
concepts_list.description: The bilinear form B(u,v) that represents the left-hand side of the weak formulation
concepts_list.justification: The bilinear form B(u,v) is introduced in the abstract form (3) as the left-hand side of the weak formulation, derived from the integrals involving gradients and strains.

concepts_list.name: linear_form_L
concepts_list.description: The linear form L(v) that represents the right-hand side of the weak formulation
concepts_list.justification: The linear form L(v) is introduced in the abstract form (3) as the right-hand side of the weak formulation, derived from the internal sources and boundary fluxes.

concepts_list.name: d_Omega
concepts_list.description: The differential element of volume in the domain Ω
concepts_list.justification: The symbol dΩ appears in the volume integrals over the domain Ω and is used to denote the volume element in the weak form.

concepts_list.name: d_Gamma
concepts_list.description: The differential element of surface area in the boundary Γ
concepts_list.justification: The symbol dΓ appears in the surface integrals over the boundary Γ and is used to denote the surface element in the weak form, particularly on Γ_t and internal interfaces.

concepts_list.name: Gamma_t
concepts_list.description: The Neumann boundary of the domain Ω where Neumann boundary conditions are prescribed
concepts_list.justification: The symbol Γ_t is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (traction) are prescribed, and is used in the weak form for the boundary integral.

new_concept_info.description:
The main computational domain in the physical space
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol Ω is explicitly defined as the domain of interest, which is a subset of R^d, and is described as the region of interest in the problem. It is also decomposed into subdomains, indicating its role as the primary domain.

The primary unknown variable (temperature or displacement) in the problem
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol u is used to denote the temperature field in the thermal problem and the displacement field in the elastostatics problem, and is referred to as the primal variable in the abstract formulation.

A test function used in the weak form of the variational problem
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol v appears in all weak formulations as a test function, and is used in the inner product with the solution u, satisfying the condition for all v in V.

The thermal conductivity tensor κ, used in the heat conduction equation
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol κ is used in the weak form as the coefficient of the gradient term in the thermal problem, and is described as a given physical parameter.

The internal heat source term f in the thermal problem
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol f is explicitly described as the heat source in the thermal boundary value problem and appears in the right-hand side of the weak form equation.

The prescribed heat flux q_bar on the boundary Γ_t
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol q_bar is introduced as the prescribed heat flux on the Neumann boundary Γ_t and appears in the boundary integral of the weak form.

The test function space V, where the test functions v are defined
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol V is introduced as the function space for the test functions, and appears in the weak form with the condition 'for all v in V'.

The differential element of volume in the domain Ω
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol dΩ appears in the volume integrals over the domain Ω and is used to denote the volume element in the weak form.

The differential element of surface area in the boundary Γ
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol dΓ appears in the surface integrals over the boundary Γ and is used to denote the surface element in the weak form, particularly on Γ_t and internal interfaces.

The Neumann boundary of the domain Ω where Neumann boundary conditions are prescribed
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol Γ_t is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (traction) are prescribed, and is used in the weak form for the boundary integral.
